collection-of-beauty-rogier-van-der-weyd: The Virgin sits enthroned beneath a Gothic canopy, holding the Christ Child who turns the page of a small book. Painted in oil on panel, the work shows van der Weyden's command of finely modelled drapery and saturated red, set against a flat gold-tooled ground in the manner of Netherlandish devotional images.
collection-of-beauty-weyden-christ-on-th: A T-shaped cross holding the crucified Christ rises before a red cloth of honour and a stone wall, flanked by the white-robed Virgin and Saint John. The reduced palette and stark spatial setting strip away narrative detail, concentrating on grief and bodily presence in a manner unusual for mid-fifteenth-century devotional painting.
collection-of-beauty-hieronymus-bosch-th: When closed, the triptych shows the world on the third day of Creation in grisaille: a translucent globe enclosing a dark, watery earth, with God depicted small in the upper left. The muted tonality contrasts sharply with the saturated colour of the interior panels, framing the work as an unfolding cosmological drama.
collection-of-beauty-sandro-botticelli-m: The enthroned Virgin nurses the Child between Saint John the Baptist and Saint John the Evangelist, set within a vaulted niche dense with lilies, palms and myrtle. Painted for the Bardi chapel in Santo Spirito, Florence, the panel pairs Botticelli's linear elegance with an unusually botanical, symbol-laden setting.
collection-of-beauty-albrecht-durer-hare: A young hare crouches alert against a blank ground, its fur built up in fine watercolour and bodycolour strokes that distinguish guard hairs from softer underfur. Among the most exacting nature studies of the Northern Renaissance, the sheet even records the cross-barred reflection of a studio window in the animal's eye.
collection-of-beauty-correggio-ritratto-: A standing woman in dark mourning dress holds a bowl in a wooded landscape, her gaze direct and reserved. Signed with the Latinised "Anton. Laet.", the portrait is sometimes identified as Veronica Gambara and marks Correggio's transition into a more refined, psychologically attentive manner.
collection-of-beauty-patenierchristopher: Saint Christopher wades across a wide river bearing the Christ Child on his shoulder, dwarfed by a panoramic landscape of jagged rocks, distant towns and luminous water. The sweeping high viewpoint is characteristic of Patinir, often credited as the first specialist in Western landscape painting.
collection-of-beauty-venus-and-organist-: Venus reclines nude on a draped bed, her small dog beside her, while an organist turns from his keyboard to gaze at her body. Part of a series Titian produced for European courts, the composition stages a debate between sight and sound as competing routes to the experience of beauty.
collection-of-beauty-venus-and-adonis-by: Venus, seen from behind, twists to embrace the youthful hunter Adonis as he pulls away with his hounds towards the hunt that will kill him. One of the mythological poesie made for Philip II of Spain, the canvas exemplifies Titian's late, freely brushed handling of flesh and landscape.
collection-of-beauty-tizian-danae-receiv: Danaë reclines nude on her bed as Jupiter descends as a shower of gold, while an old nursemaid stretches up an apron to catch the falling coins. Among Titian's poesie for the Spanish court, the scene contrasts youthful flesh and wizened greed in loose, atmospheric paint.
collection-of-beauty-the-triumph-of-deat: An army of skeletons sweeps across a scorched landscape, herding the living towards a vast coffin while gallows, wheels and burning ships fill the horizon. The panel translates the late-medieval danse macabre into a panoramic moral allegory, indebted to Bosch but rendered with Bruegel's denser observation of human types.
collection-of-beauty-canestra-di-frutta-: A wicker basket of grapes, figs, peaches and a withered apple sits at eye level on a bare ledge, projecting slightly into the viewer's space. Often cited as the first autonomous still life in Italian painting, the work is unsparing about wormholes and decay; its plain ground is a later overpaint.
collection-of-beauty-circle-of-pieter-br: Villagers skate and slide on a frozen river beside snow-covered houses, while at right a propped board baited with grain forms a trap for unwary birds. One of around a hundred surviving versions after Pieter Bruegel the Elder's composition, it pairs winter genre with a quiet emblem of human precariousness.
collection-of-beauty-pedro-pablo-rubens-: Susanna twists away from two leering elders who press in from the shadows of her garden bath, her startled body and rumpled drapery filling the canvas. Painted shortly after Rubens's return from Italy, the picture fuses Venetian colour with the muscular Roman figures he had studied there.
collection-of-beauty-peter-paul-rubens-l: Inside a shadowed rocky grotto, Lot's two daughters ply their elderly father with wine, the burning city of Sodom glowing in the distance. The compact, candle-lit composition is typical of Rubens's early Antwerp years, when Caravaggesque tenebrism still inflected his treatment of biblical narrative.
collection-of-beauty-peter-paul-rubens-d: A small child, often identified as Rubens's nephew Philip, looks out from a softly lit ground while gently holding a bird in one hand. The intimate scale and warm flesh tones place the panel within the painter's family portraits, a private counterpart to the grand commissions for which he is better known.
collection-of-beauty-velazquez-el-triunf: A pale, half-nude Bacchus crowns a kneeling soldier with vine leaves while a circle of weather-beaten Spanish drinkers grins out at the viewer. Painted in Madrid before Velázquez's first trip to Italy, the canvas pulls classical mythology down to tavern level with a sober, almost documentary realism.
collection-of-beauty-hercules-lucha-con-: Hercules grapples with the many-headed Hydra of Lerna, his muscular body twisting against the dark serpentine coils. The canvas belongs to a series of ten Labours of Hercules painted for the Hall of Realms in the Buen Retiro Palace, where Zurbarán's stark tenebrism set the hero against an almost theatrical void.
collection-of-beauty-landschaft-mit-kuhe: A pastoral panorama unfolds along a wooded riverbank, with cattle watered in the foreground and duck hunters moving through the reeds beyond. Painted in the artist's final years at his country estate Het Steen, the work belongs to a group of expansive landscapes notable for their loose handling and golden, atmospheric light.
collection-of-beauty-velazquez-de-breda-: Justin of Nassau hands the keys of the Dutch city to the Genoese general Ambrogio Spinola, who lays a conciliatory hand on the defeated commander's shoulder. The forest of upright Spanish pikes that gives the painting its nickname, Las Lanzas, organises the right half of the composition, painted for the Hall of Realms in the Buen Retiro.
collection-of-beauty-landschaft-mit-turm: A small panel showing a fortified tower set on a wooded rise, approached by a slender bridge under the warm light of a lowering sun. The intimate scale and rapid, fluid brushwork are characteristic of the late landscape sketches Rubens made around his estate at Elewijt.
collection-of-beauty-peter-paul-rubens-c: The apostle is shown crucified upside down at his own request, his contorted, foreshortened body filling a tall altarpiece format. Commissioned for St Peter's Church in Cologne, the work was among the artist's last major altarpieces, completed shortly before his death and dispatched to the parish where his father had once lived.
collection-of-beauty-san-jeronimo-leyend: The Church Father is depicted half-length in a rocky grotto, bare-chested and absorbed in his book, a crucifix steadying his left hand. Murillo treats the penitent scholar with restrained naturalism, the warm Sevillian light modelling weathered skin and beard against a deep brown ground.
collection-of-beauty-adoration-of-the-sh: Shepherds press forward to venerate the naked Christ Child held by the Virgin, a lamb bound at the foreground as a foreshadowing of sacrifice. The earthy palette and tender, everyday types reflect Murillo's early debt to Ribera and the Spanish naturalist tradition before his manner softened in later years.
collection-of-beauty-bartolome-esteban-p: Joseph stoops to play with the infant Jesus and a small bird while Mary pauses at her spinning, the goldfinch a traditional emblem of the Passion to come. The domestic warmth and soft chiaroscuro are typical of Murillo's Sevillian devotional pictures, which translated sacred subjects into the rhythms of family life.
collection-of-beauty-mercurius-en-argus-: An engraving after Jordaens showing Mercury raising his sword above the slumbering hundred-eyed giant Argus, with Io transformed into a cow at the left. The print belongs to the wide diffusion of Jordaens' mythologies through reproductive engraving, here cataloged in Hollstein as Dutch 7.
collection-of-beauty-murillo-descanso-en: The Holy Family pauses on its journey into Egypt, Mary cradling the sleeping Christ Child while Joseph watches over them and the donkey grazes nearby. Now in the Hermitage, the picture exemplifies Murillo's mature estilo vaporoso, in which figures emerge from a hazy, golden atmosphere with notably softened contours.
collection-of-beauty-bartolome-esteban-m: The first canonised saint of the Americas kneels in the white and black habit of a Dominican tertiary, gazing on the Christ Child seated on a cushion above her workbasket. Murillo presents the Limeña mystic with the gentle sweetness and silvery half-light that defined his late religious painting.
collection-of-beauty-peinturesmuseefabre: Venus leans toward Vulcan in his forge to plead for arms for her son Aeneas, the Cyclopes labouring at the anvil behind. Submitted as the painter's morceau de réception to the Académie Royale in 1734, the picture is a polished demonstration of the decorative Rococo mythology Natoire would soon deploy at Versailles.
collection-of-beauty-elisabeth-vigee-le-: An allegorical pair: Peace, in classical drapery, gently leads forward Abundance, who carries a sheaf of wheat and a cornucopia. The painting was Vigée Le Brun's reception piece for the Académie Royale, accepted in 1783, and shows her combining Neoclassical subject matter with the warm flesh tones and silken finish of her portrait practice.
collection-of-beauty-the-muscles-of-the-: An anatomical engraving combining a flayed hand after Bernhard Siegfried Albinus with a foot after De Courcelles, the muscles, tendons and bones laid bare for study. Engraved by Robert Bénard for the late eighteenth-century editions of the Encyclopédie, the plate typifies the Enlightenment project of consolidating earlier anatomical illustration into a single compendium.
collection-of-beauty-elisabeth-louise-vi: The actress of the Comédie-Italienne turns toward the viewer in a fur-trimmed coat and broad black hat, a muff held loosely at her side. Vigée Le Brun catches the sitter mid-movement with a fresh, animated brushwork that announces the informal, theatrical portraiture of the late ancien régime.

A double portrait set out of doors, the Marquise de Pezay seated beside the Marquise de Rougé, whose two young sons press in beside her. Painted on the eve of the Revolution, the canvas blends the natural settings and tender maternal grouping that Vigée Le Brun had refined in her portraits of Marie Antoinette and her circle.
collection-of-beauty-wilhelm-alexander-w: A panoramic battle scene of the 1807 siege of Cosel in Silesia, Bavarian troops repulsing a Prussian sortie across an open foreground while the fortress smokes in the distance. Kobell, court painter in Munich, here works in the large-format Schlachtenbild tradition, organising hundreds of small figures within a clear, brightly lit landscape.
collection-of-beauty-suzanna-en-de-ouder: A Neoclassical treatment of the biblical episode in which the chaste Susanna, bathing in her garden, is spied upon by two elders concealed in foliage. The smooth handling of the nude and theatrical contrast of light and shadow reflect the Flemish painter's training in the academic tradition of David.
collection-of-beauty-1280px-rocky-landsc: A study of the Neurathen rock formation in Saxon Switzerland, with weathered sandstone pillars rising against a pale sky. Friedrich drew repeatedly from this region near Dresden, translating direct observation into the meditative, vertical compositions characteristic of his mature Romantic landscapes.
collection-of-beauty-stieler-auguste-str: A half-length portrait of Auguste Strobl, later Auguste Hilber, painted for King Ludwig I of Bavaria's Gallery of Beauties at Nymphenburg. Stieler's polished oil technique, soft modelling and restrained palette typify the Biedermeier court portraiture commissioned for that celebrated cycle of Munich women.
collection-of-beauty-anna-hillmayer-1829: Anna Hillmayer, daughter of a Munich game merchant, sat for this picture as part of Ludwig I's Gallery of Beauties. Stieler departs from aristocratic convention to record a tradesman's daughter with the same idealised serenity and porcelain finish he gave his royal sitters.
collection-of-beauty-keisai-eisen-wasser: A coiling water dragon, or mizuchi, from a late ukiyo-e series devoted to birds, beasts, insects and fish. Eisen, better known for bijin-ga prints of Edo women, here adapts the colour woodblock to a more decorative, naturalist mode in keeping with kacho-e tradition.
collection-of-beauty-stieler-marianna-ma: The Italian noblewoman, translator and longtime confidante of Ludwig I of Bavaria, shown with the soft idealisation reserved for the king's Gallery of Beauties in Munich. Stieler emphasises her dark eyes and intellectual composure, signalling her standing as a scholar rather than mere society sitter.
collection-of-beauty-vincent-van-gogh-18: A nocturnal view of a yellow-awninged café on the Place du Forum in Arles, with cobblestones receding under a sky of large stars. Van Gogh painted it without using black, building the night entirely from saturated yellows, blues and greens — an early experiment in his ambition to render the south at night in pure colour.
collection-of-beauty-jean-leon-gerome-th: Dawn in the Bois de Boulogne: a wounded Pierrot collapses into the arms of the Duc de Guise while a Venetian doge inspects the injury and a black-cloaked Domino reels away. Gérôme's tightly finished, stage-like composition, derived from a version made for the Duc d'Aumale, exemplifies his blend of academic precision and theatrical narrative.
collection-of-beauty-prado-los-desastres: Plate 4 of the Disasters of War, captioned 'The women give courage'. Goya shows female combatants resisting French troops during the Peninsular War, the etching and aquatint biting through the page in his characteristically stark contrasts of black and gnawed white.

Plate 46 of the Disasters of War, 'This is bad'. A friar is set upon amid scattered bodies, the scene drawn with the cramped, shallow space and bitten line that give the series its grim moral force. The plates were withheld until decades after Goya's death.

Plate 47 of the Disasters of War, 'This is how it happened'. A clergyman is dragged and despoiled in the chaos of war, Goya pairing terse caption and unsparing image in the documentary mode that distinguishes the series from any heroic battle tradition.
collection-of-beauty-madame-monet-au-can: Camille Doncieux, the painter's first wife, reclining on a sofa in quiet thought. The intimate domestic subject and muted tonal range belong to Monet's early-1870s figure painting, before his work shifted decisively outdoors toward pure landscape and atmospheric study.
collection-of-beauty-monet-the-thames-at: A view across the Thames toward the Houses of Parliament and Westminster Bridge, dissolved in river mist. Painted during Monet's wartime exile in London, the canvas anticipates by three decades his later Thames series, already setting silhouetted architecture against a pearly, fog-bound sky.
collection-of-beauty-la-debacle-monet-w5: The breakup of ice on the Seine after a hard winter near Vétheuil, with floes drifting past bare riverbanks. Monet returned to this motif repeatedly in 1880, using broken brushwork to register the shifting interplay of water, ice and reflected sky.
collection-of-beauty-gustave-dore-mont-s: A panoramic view in the Vosges of the wooded heights of Mont Sainte-Odile, ringed by the prehistoric drystone enclosure known as the Pagan Wall. Better remembered for his book illustrations, Doré here works at large scale in oil, treating his native Alsace with a dark Romantic grandeur.
collection-of-beauty-gustave-dore-st-joh: Death rides forth as the pale horseman of Revelation, illustrating Saint John's vision in the Apocalypse. The print belongs to Doré's widely circulated Bible illustrations, where dense wood-engraved hatching and theatrical lighting gave Victorian readers their dominant visual image of scripture.
collection-of-beauty-claude-monet-die-we: A willow standing on the bank of the Epte near Giverny, caught in the pale, cool light of a spring morning. The pastel palette and feathery brushwork are typical of Monet's mid-1880s riverside studies, in which he began to anchor compositions around a single tree as a recurring motif.
collection-of-beauty-les-soleils-jardin-: A dense bed of sunflowers fills the canvas, painted in the artist's own garden at Petit Gennevilliers on the banks of the Seine. The loose handling and saturated yellows reflect a late shift toward Impressionist plein-air practice, after he had largely turned from urban subjects to horticulture.
collection-of-beauty-leve-de-lune-sur-le: From the series One Hundred Aspects of the Moon, the print shows the warlord Cao Cao gazing at the moon rising over Mount Nanping on the eve of the Battle of Red Cliffs. Yoshitoshi distils the Chinese narrative into a spare vertical composition, a hallmark of his late ukiyo-e woodblock work.
collection-of-beauty-2022-06-24-at-13-41: A bare lane leads into the village of Giverny under winter light, with leafless trees and pale fields rendered in muted blues and ochres. Painted shortly after Monet settled in Giverny, the canvas catalogued as Wildenstein 967 belongs to a small group of winter views recording his new surroundings.
collection-of-beauty-vincent-van-gogh-en: A walled wheatfield is shown at sunrise, the sun cresting low above distant hills while curling brushstrokes animate the grain. Painted from the window of his room at the asylum in Saint-Rémy-de-Provence, it is one of a sustained series in which the same enclosed field is observed across seasons and times of day.
collection-of-beauty-lion-tentant-de-sai: A lion stretches from a rocky perch to bat at a butterfly, the towering predator turned momentarily playful. Gérôme treats the animal with the precise anatomical finish of his academic training, while the desert setting reflects the Orientalist subjects he favoured in his late career.
collection-of-beauty-thestarrynightbyvin: A swirling night sky churns over a quiet village dominated by a dark cypress and a slender church spire. Painted from the artist's room at the Saint-Paul-de-Mausole asylum in Saint-Rémy, it reworks the view from his window with thick impasto and rhythmic, spiralling brushwork.
collection-of-beauty-jean-leon-gerome-la: Greek warriors in plumed helmets perform the ancient pyrrhic war dance before a seated audience, swords and shields raised in unison. The carefully staged classical reconstruction is typical of Gérôme's late academic manner, in which archaeological detail is mobilised to bring antiquity to vivid life.
collection-of-beauty-aphonse-mucha-rose-: A young woman wreathed in roses stands within a decorative arch, her flowing hair and gown merging with the surrounding blooms. Part of Mucha's Flowers series of four panel decorations, the design exemplifies the Art Nouveau idiom he refined in Paris in the late 1890s.
collection-of-beauty-blauwe-irissen-rp-p: A cluster of blue irises rises against a plain ground in a tall, narrow format suited to the Japanese hashira-e pillar print. Designed by the shin-hanga artist Ohara Koson, the colour woodcut combines the close natural observation of kachō-e bird-and-flower prints with early twentieth-century printmaking technique.
kunstformen-images-haeckel-ascomycetes: Plate from Kunstformen der Natur showing a symmetrical arrangement of sac fungi, with cup-shaped fruiting bodies and powdery mildews magnified and framed like ornament. Haeckel's lithographs translated microscopic biology into decorative compositions that fed directly into Art Nouveau design.
collection-of-beauty-claude-monet-the-wa: Pale lilies float on a luminous pond crossed by the trailing reflections of weeping willows. The panel forms part of the Grandes Décorations cycle Monet painted at Giverny for the oval rooms of the Musée de l'Orangerie in Paris, where pond, sky and bank dissolve into a continuous surface.
collection-of-beauty-leonardo-da-vinci-v: Saint Anne supports the Virgin on her lap as Mary reaches for the Christ child, who plays with a lamb foreshadowing the Passion. Left unfinished at the artist's death and now in the Louvre, the panel is celebrated for its pyramidal grouping, soft sfumato modelling and atmospheric mountainous landscape.
collection-of-beauty-lowenjagd-peter-pau: Mounted hunters and lions tangle in violent diagonal motion, men and beasts pulled into a single churning mass. This small oil sketch, held by the Alte Pinakothek in Munich, is a preparatory modello for one of Rubens's larger hunt scenes, with the brushwork left openly visible.
collection-of-beauty-rogier-van-der-weyd: Christ on the Cross occupies the central panel, flanked by donor figures and saints in the wings, set against gilded and architectural backgrounds. The triptych shows the precise drapery, restrained gesture and devotional intensity characteristic of fifteenth-century Netherlandish panel painting.
